behaviors.description: Reject invalid HMAC signature with 401 and do not forward body.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (auth check -> enqueue path), not just hash math. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network requirement beyond in-process app wiring. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text is about "signature does not match" and "body is not forwarded," which is enforceable with in-process HTTP + queue spy.
behaviors.test_description: Run the HTTP handler with a real middleware/router stack and a test queue adapter. Send a request with a bad `X-Signature-256`; assert 401 and assert queue `enqueue` was never called.

behaviors.description: Persist event to queue before returning HTTP 200.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bug appears across handler + persistent queue write + response path. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with local persistent queue (DB/filesystem) in-process; no external network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text only requires write-before-ack ordering, not real network faults/OS timing primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Use real queue persistence module (test DB/file). Make enqueue intentionally block until commit point is observable; verify 200 is not emitted until durable write completes, then verify record exists.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing completion.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug is in async handoff boundary (enqueue/dispatch vs processor completion). REAL-INFRA: no real external infra is required; a controllable long-running processor promise is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text targets wait semantics, not real OS/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with real dispatch path and a downstream processor that resolves after a delay. Assert HTTP response is sent before processor completion signal.

behaviors.description: Response arrives within 3 seconds under slow concurrent downstream load.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this is wall-clock SLO under concurrency; event-loop scheduling, socket handling, and real HTTP timing can hide failures in mocks. MOCK-HIDE: fake timers/stubs can miss contention/backpressure behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly requires "within 3 seconds wall-clock time" under concurrent slow processing.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real service process, generate concurrent webhook traffic while earlier events are processed slowly, and measure end-to-end HTTP latency over real sockets; assert responses stay under 3s.

behaviors.description: Do not enqueue duplicate event IDs.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedup correctness depends on handler->queue state handoff and persistent uniqueness/state check. REAL-INFRA: local persistence is sufficient; no external network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected Unit because bug can occur only when request path interacts with stored prior IDs.
behaviors.test_description: Send the same event ID twice through the handler using real queue persistence. Assert first request writes once; second request does not create another queue entry.

behaviors.description: If queue reports full, return 503 with Retry-After.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is mapping of queue backpressure signal to HTTP status/header across module boundary. REAL-INFRA: does not require real OS/network/subprocess; deterministic full signal from queue module is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text says "when the queue reports it is full" (signal handling), not real capacity race timing.
behaviors.test_description: Use handler + queue adapter that returns `full` condition; assert HTTP 503 and `Retry-After` header are set correctly and no enqueue success path is taken.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects overflow.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears in queue storage/config enforcement, not just caller logic. REAL-INFRA: requires real queue persistence module (local DB/filesystem) but not external network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected Unit because capacity enforcement is an interaction of config + persistence behavior; rejected System because no real external OS/network condition is stated beyond local storage use.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue with a small capacity, enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one more and assert explicit rejection/error without corrupting prior entries.
